steps.title: Find the gesture (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch one long diagonal line for the spine of the rod — from lower-left up to upper-right, with a very slight bend near the tip. This single line is the whole rod.

steps.title: Mark the handle and reel (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, mark a short thicker section at the bottom of the diagonal for the handle grip, and sketch a small circle just above it for the reel.

steps.title: Plan the guide loops (pencil)
steps.description: Place three or four tiny dots along the upper portion of the rod where the line guides will sit. They get closer together as they approach the tip.

steps.title: Switch to ink and lay down the rod
steps.description: Switch to ink. Draw the rod as one long confident stroke from the handle to the tip, moving from your shoulder rather than your wrist. A second parallel stroke close beside it gives the rod its thickness, tapering toward the tip.

steps.title: Ink the handle and reel (ink)
steps.description: Still in ink, trace the handle grip as a chunky rectangle with rounded ends, and the reel as a small circle attached to the rod with a short stem. A short straight crank handle sticking out of the reel finishes it.

steps.title: Ink the guide loops (ink)
steps.description: At each dot you placed, draw a small oval or U-shape around the rod for a line guide. Each one is just two short strokes.
